ops.body:
Spike complete (CC 2.1.207, scratch profile — CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR + CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR both overridden; both are required for isolation).

(a) Fresh install: GO. `claude plugin install depender@spikemkt` auto-installs AND auto-enables captain-hook at the same scope ("+ 1 dependency: captain-hook") — but ONLY if the captain-hook marketplace is already added; it is NOT auto-bootstrapped. Without it: plugin installs but fails to load entirely (skills dark). Adding the marketplace afterward heals it.

(b) In-place update: NO-GO via `claude plugin update` — it bumps the version and silently leaves a newly-added dependency unresolved (errors=None, no warning; plugin keeps loading). Recovery = re-run `claude plugin install` (reports "already installed" but pulls + enables the dependency) or /reload-plugins in-session.

(c) Unknown marketplace / missing allowlist: dep declared in a MARKETPLACE ENTRY + missing allowlist → hard install failure (fail closed). Dep declared in PLUGIN.JSON + missing allowlist → installs, loads, dependency silently skipped (fail OPEN, no signal). Unresolvable dep at install/load time → whole plugin "failed to load", its own skills included.

GO/NO-GO: dependency-floor contract works for new users with a documented 2-step install (marketplace add, then install). Existing installs must NOT be told `plugin update` — READMEs get marketplace add + re-run install. allowCrossMarketplaceDependenciesOn is load-bearing (fail-open without it) → `pack lint` enforces it. Fleet users already running captain-hook are unaffected (dep already satisfied at update time). Evidence boundary: /reload-plugins and background auto-update paths answered from docs only (unauthenticated scratch profile); all CLI paths directly observed. Fixtures + logs: session scratchpad dep-spike/.
